title: [BUG] Cannot merge already merged cells after insert / duplicate a new row
author: Louis-Ni
body: ## 🐛 Bug Report Lib version: ^4.3.0 ## Steps To Reproduce ```javascript const startRow = 10 let counter = 0 item.forEach((item: any) => { worksheet.insertRow(prescrionStartAt+counter, [1, 2, 3, 4]) worksheet.mergeCells(`A${startRow+counter}:D${startRow+counter}`) counter++ }); ``` the lines I added exceed line 12, it will cause an error, it's ok if I add 1 line below 10 but the **item** is dynamic, I don't know how many lines should be added. I had searched for a solution like: [issues#1567](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1567#issuecomment-801428380) but I can't find the property on the `sheet.model.merges` ## The expected behaviour: it should be added for `n` new rows into the sheet, but it caused an error when my Excel temple has a merged line below line 10 Does anyone have ideas to fix this one?

comments.author: MGber
comments.body: I've addressed the bug topic in this comment: [link](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1567#issuecomment-1677355538).

comments.author: dinhhai96-vn
comments.body: The attributes assigned to cells in Excel are automatically assigned to that cell. You need to unmerge them first and then use the attributes you want. For example: A2-A4 is being merged, you want to add a line above, you need to unmerge and then add the line. Finally, find the location and merge it.

comments.author: imfurankase
IonelLupu
comments.body:
I am still getting `Cannot merge already merged cells` even after using the `moveMergesModel` helper above. 😞  I even used [this approach](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/discussions/1967#discussioncomment-2172573) of unmerging the cells before merging them. Getting the same error. 

What can be done?
